scenario: Kestrel Logistics, a carrier handling 18000 shipments per day, configures a Camunda 8.8 REST outbound connector to call a slow customs API that sometimes takes 90 seconds to respond. The integration team must allow an unlimited read wait time for these responses but keep a fast 5 second connection timeout so the worker fails quickly when the host is unreachable.
question: Which Connection timeout and Read timeout values satisfy these two requirements?

options.text: Set Connection timeout to 0 and Read timeout to 5, where 0 disables the connect attempt entirely and 5 caps the read phase at 5 seconds for every customs API call
Set Connection timeout to -1 and Read timeout to -1, where -1 is the documented sentinel for waiting indefinitely on either phase regardless of the default behaviour
Set Connection timeout to 5 and Read timeout to 0, where 0 allows an unlimited wait time for slow responses on the read side and 5 caps the connect phase at 5 seconds
Set Connection timeout to 5 and Read timeout to 90, matching the worst observed customs API response time so any longer call fails the job immediately at the read phase

a.text: Incorrect. The roles are reversed: setting Connection timeout to 0 means an unlimited wait for the connection, not a disabled connect, and Read timeout 5 caps the read phase at 5 seconds.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented sentinel for an unlimited wait time is 0, not -1; the default for both Connection timeout and Read timeout is 20 seconds when no value is specified.

c.text: Correct. Read timeout 0 allows an unlimited wait for slow responses on the read side, and Connection timeout 5 caps the connection-establishment phase at 5 seconds so unreachable hosts fail fast.

d.text: Incorrect. Read timeout 90 caps the read phase at 90 seconds and would fail any longer call; the requirement is an unlimited read wait, which the docs achieve only by setting the value to 0.

explanation: Connection timeout and Read timeout default to 20 seconds. Setting either to 0 allows an unlimited wait. Connection timeout governs establishing the connection to the server; Read timeout governs reading data from the server once the connection is open.
